SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.config

Record Class ExecutionOptions

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.config.ExecutionOptions
Record Components:
batchSize - Number of rows processed per batch (default 8192)
coalesceBatches - Whether to coalesce small batches into larger ones
collectStatistics - Whether to collect statistics during query execution
targetPartitions - Number of target partitions for repartitioning (default: CPU cores)
timeZone - Default time zone for temporal operations (default "+00:00")
planningConcurrency - Number of threads to use for planning concurrency
skipPhysicalAggregateSchemaCheck - Whether to skip the physical aggregate schema check
spillCompression - Compression codec used for spilling intermediate results to disk
sortSpillReservationBytes - Memory reserved for sort spill operations in bytes
sortInPlaceThresholdBytes - Threshold below which sorts are performed in-place (in bytes)
maxSpillFileSizeBytes - Maximum spill file size in bytes
metaFetchConcurrency - Number of concurrent metadata fetch operations
minimumParallelOutputFiles - Minimum number of parallel output files
softMaxRowsPerOutputFile - Soft maximum number of rows per output file
maxBufferedBatchesPerOutputFile - Maximum number of buffered batches per output file
listingTableIgnoreSubdirectory - Whether to ignore subdirectories when listing table files
listingTableFactoryInferPartitions - Whether to infer partitions from directory structure
enableRecursiveCtes - Whether to enable recursive common table expressions
splitFileGroupsByStatistics - Whether to split file groups by statistics for better parallelism
keepPartitionByColumns - Whether to keep partition-by columns in query output
skipPartialAggregationProbeRatioThreshold - Probe ratio threshold for skipping partial aggregation
skipPartialAggregationProbeRowsThreshold - Probe rows threshold for skipping partial aggregation
useRowNumberEstimatesToOptimizePartitioning - Whether to use row number estimates to optimize partitioning
enforceBatchSizeInJoins - Whether to enforce batch size limits in join operations
objectstoreWriterBufferSize - Buffer size for object store writer in bytes
enableAnsiMode - Whether to enable ANSI SQL mode
perfectHashJoinSmallBuildThreshold - Maximum entries for "small" side in a perfect hash join
perfectHashJoinMinKeyDensity - Minimum key density for perfect hash join eligibility
parquet - Parquet-specific options
public record ExecutionOptions(Integer batchSize, Boolean coalesceBatches, Boolean collectStatistics, Integer targetPartitions, String timeZone, Integer planningConcurrency, Boolean skipPhysicalAggregateSchemaCheck, SpillCompression spillCompression, Long sortSpillReservationBytes, Long sortInPlaceThresholdBytes, Long maxSpillFileSizeBytes, Integer metaFetchConcurrency, Integer minimumParallelOutputFiles, Long softMaxRowsPerOutputFile, Integer maxBufferedBatchesPerOutputFile, Boolean listingTableIgnoreSubdirectory, Boolean listingTableFactoryInferPartitions, Boolean enableRecursiveCtes, Boolean splitFileGroupsByStatistics, Boolean keepPartitionByColumns, Double skipPartialAggregationProbeRatioThreshold, Integer skipPartialAggregationProbeRowsThreshold, Boolean useRowNumberEstimatesToOptimizePartitioning, Boolean enforceBatchSizeInJoins, Long objectstoreWriterBufferSize, Boolean enableAnsiMode, Integer perfectHashJoinSmallBuildThreshold, Double perfectHashJoinMinKeyDensity, ParquetOptions parquet) extends Record
Execution configuration options. Maps to DataFusion's ExecutionOptions.

All fields are nullable. A null value means the DataFusion default is used.

Example: 

ExecutionOptions exec = ExecutionOptions.builder()
    .batchSize(4096)
    .targetPartitions(8)
    .coalesceBatches(true)
    .build();
ConfigOptions config = ConfigOptions.builder()
    .execution(exec)
    .build();
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionOptions

      public ExecutionOptions(Integer batchSize, Boolean coalesceBatches, Boolean collectStatistics, Integer targetPartitions, String timeZone, Integer planningConcurrency, Boolean skipPhysicalAggregateSchemaCheck, SpillCompression spillCompression, Long sortSpillReservationBytes, Long sortInPlaceThresholdBytes, Long maxSpillFileSizeBytes, Integer metaFetchConcurrency, Integer minimumParallelOutputFiles, Long softMaxRowsPerOutputFile, Integer maxBufferedBatchesPerOutputFile, Boolean listingTableIgnoreSubdirectory, Boolean listingTableFactoryInferPartitions, Boolean enableRecursiveCtes, Boolean splitFileGroupsByStatistics, Boolean keepPartitionByColumns, Double skipPartialAggregationProbeRatioThreshold, Integer skipPartialAggregationProbeRowsThreshold, Boolean useRowNumberEstimatesToOptimizePartitioning, Boolean enforceBatchSizeInJoins, Long objectstoreWriterBufferSize, Boolean enableAnsiMode, Integer perfectHashJoinSmallBuildThreshold, Double perfectHashJoinMinKeyDensity, ParquetOptions parquet)

  • Method Details

    • builder

      public static ExecutionOptions.Builder builder()
      Returns a new builder.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
